model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase representational capacity with a wider backbone and classifier head so the model can fit CIFAR-10 patterns better within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"The warmup+cosine schedule likely under-trains in a short 256-step run; keeping a constant Adam learning rate should improve optimization efficiency for this small fixed budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps, a higher Adam learning rate should speed early progress and can reduce validation loss if the current setting is under-updating.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Switch to AdamW to decouple weight decay from adaptive moments, which often improves generalization at the same learning rate in short-budget CNN training.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"AdamW regressed versus Adam in this run, so reverting to Adam restores the stronger optimizer configuration before testing further changes.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Add one extra residual block at fixed width to increase feature refinement depth with minimal architectural complexity, which can improve validation loss under the short training budget.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","new":"        self.blocks = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(channels, channels, 3, padding=1, bias=bias),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(channels) if USE_BATCHNORM else nn.Identity(),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),
        )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The added residual block slightly worsened validation loss, so reverting to the single-block design restores the stronger architecture before further tuning.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With fixed 256 steps, doubling batch size exposes the model to more unique examples per run, which can improve validation loss in this budget-limited setting.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"After increasing batch size to 128, a moderately higher learning rate often improves optimization speed and can further reduce validation loss within the same fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In fixed-step training, increasing batch size to 256 can cover nearly the full dataset within budget and may improve validation loss by reducing gradient noise while preserving update count.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch size 256 regressed and is much slower, so reverting to 128 restores the better-performing and more efficient fixed-step configuration.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Slightly reducing weight decay can improve fit in this short fixed-step regime while keeping regularization, potentially lowering validation loss from the current strong baseline.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The recent configuration is improving steadily; a small learning-rate increase may extract more progress within 256 steps without changing model complexity.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Loss has improved with recent learning-rate increases, so a small additional step to 2e-3 may further improve optimization within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.8e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Learning rate 2e-3 regressed, so reverting to 1.8e-3 restores the better-performing setting before further tuning.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Lowering Adam's second beta to 0.99 makes variance estimates adapt faster, which can improve short-horizon optimization under a fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The beta change regressed, so reverting to (0.9, 0.999) restores the stronger Adam dynamics before final tuning steps.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Increase classifier head capacity slightly to improve class separation while keeping the convolutional backbone unchanged and computational overhead modest.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The larger classifier head regressed, so reverting FC_HIDDEN to 64 restores the best-performing configuration for the final checkpoint.","secondary_modes":[]}
